title: Chapter Sixty-Five: The Two Flame Formation
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

A month inside the Blood Flame Sect.

The Seventh Apostle showed him everything. True qi operation, footwork, the movement of every muscle fibre in her body technique. She demonstrated with the abandon of a woman who believed her prisoner would never leave, and the fervour of a fanatic who worshipped creation itself.

"You won't make martial arts just for me?" she hissed one evening, her hand suddenly seizing his head, red eye blazing. "Really? You dare?"

"Let go. I was unravelling your base blood technique verses."

"Ah."

The grip vanished. She pulled back, face crumpling into something almost like remorse, and began stroking both sides of his head with both hands.

"Sorry. Sorry. I interrupted sacred work. Sorry."

He was already used to it.

What mattered was the walk.

Every day, he asked to see the settlement. To observe. To study the layout, the patrol routes, the rhythm of the city's masters. The Seventh Apostle obliged with a smile, tugging his sleeve like a child showing off a beloved garden.

*She's looking down on me.*

On the fifth day, he asked about the outskirts.

"Want to find clues to break it?" she asked brightly.

The Two Flame Blood Spirit Formation. Hundreds of years old, continuously strengthened, layer upon layer of sorcerous architecture. The entire village wrapped in a barrier -- rocks, grass, trees, all filled with qi and bound together in an arrangement that created concealment close to spatial severance. Cut off from the outside. Only the wind moved freely through the twisted space.

*Plant energy.*

He felt it through his innate energy sense. The formation drew power from everything it touched -- from the natural world itself, woven into a structure so vast that no single martial artist could break it.

But Yeon-shin was not thinking about breaking it.

He was thinking about what would happen if he planted something inside it.

Blue lightning struck behind his eyes. Inspiration, white-hot -- the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm, the sword song, formation theory. Three techniques rotating together, expanding, refining, fusing into something new.

Every day during the walks, he pressed his palm against a pillar, a wall, a cornerstone. Brief contact. Tiny filaments of blue qi threaded into the formation's architecture like seeds pressed into soil. The Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm's cultivation path -- designed to unravel blood technique qi at a fundamental level -- planted deep, invisible, dormant.

The Seventh Apostle saw nothing. Her confidence was absolute. Hundreds of years of safety had bred complacency as thick as the walls themselves.

*They look at me like a spirit beast held in their hands.*

He thought of what would happen if those seeds all detonated at once. Every filament of palm technique qi igniting simultaneously within the formation. Not breaking the barrier from without. Devouring it from within.

The crown throbbed with familiar agony. He did not flinch.

"Isn't it wonderful?" the Seventh Apostle said beside him, gazing at the shimmering distortion at the village's edge. "A thousand-year barrier. Can you feel that it's not the time to distinguish whether you're poison or medicine?"

Yeon-shin half-closed his eyes. Dimmed the blue light that tried to leak from his gaze.

Let her see despair. Let her see a caged animal accepting its cage.

*For now.*

---

Far to the south, in a government office in Huizhou, Hyeon Won-chang raged.

"This is why major sects cannot be trusted!"

He seemed unaware that Wei Ji Myo-hwa of the Zhongnan Sect stood directly in front of him. Her expression was equally grim.

"The Namgung family did save face," she said carefully. "They acknowledged Lightning Flash's merit. They gave you the spirit pill."

"If you hadn't been there, we would have received nothing! Petty cowards!"

Twenty days since Clear Night Valley. Twenty days of searching -- Myo-hwa combing through Chizhou and Ninguo while Won-chang recovered enough to move. They had found nothing. The Blood Flame Sect's main hall had swallowed Jeong Yeon-shin whole.

The door opened.

Cheong Myeong stood in the frame. His beautiful face was rigid, all warmth gone. Frost seemed to linger on his skin.

"I heard the situation," he said. "Wait. The murim will gather."

"What do you mean, gather?" Myo-hwa asked.

"Shaolin warrior monks, including Little Divine Monk Gak Jeong. Mount Hua Sect leader's disciple and Plum Blossom Sword Masters. Martial officials with government permission across South Zhili. Noble families from Huguang, Jiangxi, Henan. Evil sect rampaging is a state matter. They have no grounds to refuse."

Won-chang's eyes were red, but his voice went cold with analysis. "Even so -- it is not enough power to strike the Blood Flame Sect. Not their main hall. Even three of the Nine Sects joining would only bring part of their forces. Apostle-level enemies are a different matter entirely."

Cheong Myeong slowly shook his head.

"That level of difference does not matter."

"Why?"

The Elf was silent. His blue eyes were not warm. They were not cold. They held something that Won-chang and Myo-hwa had never seen from him before.

Fear.

"The Sect Lord is coming."

The words fell into the room like stones into deep water.

And somewhere far away, inside a hidden city ringed by ancient sorcery, Jeong Yeon-shin pressed his palm against a stone column. Blue qi sank into it like water into parched earth.

He withdrew his hand and kept walking.

The Seventh Apostle smiled beside him, seeing nothing.

The crown throbbed.

*Soon.*
